Verified Probate 850 Petition, For Order Confirming Trust Ownership Of And Conveyance Of Title To Bank Accounts
Off Calendar Jun-28-2022 Continued To Sep-13-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Emilie Calhoun and Diedre Von Rock appeared by zoom for Nancy Nowotny.
Christopher Neary appeared by zoom for Judith Bailey and Joseph Capko (respondents).

The court thanked counsel for the status reports, and stated that they are very helpful and that it did not quite understand the point Mr. Neary was making in his status report by citing 415.10. Mr. Neary stated that he understood that the court was requesting personal service at least on the disinherited beneficiaries (4 to 5 individuals), and that he did not see any evidence of personal service. Ms. Calhoun stated that there is no son of the decedent, that Mr. Neary is misrepresenting facts to the court, and that it was her understanding that because this is an 850 petition the court asked that she serve the intestate heirs and other individuals that are named in the instruments in accordance with probate code 851 a. Ms. Calhoun also stated that they have acknowledgement of receipts from all parties except for maybe one or two, that she personally served those who did not respond, and that some of the proofs of service had the incorrect case number on them, but they have filed proofs of service on everybody as of yesterday. The court raised the issue of the parties needing to participate in at least one form of ADR prior to the trial. Mr. Neary stated that they have met and conferred, and that they have agreed to a mediator and to a date in late August or September. The court informed counsel that it will be issuing a case management order. Ms. Calhoun stated that she is waiting for a law and motion matter regarding Bank of America, suggested putting the matter out at least three months, and stated that they have to hire an expert witness, that they need to take some depositions and suggested a trial maybe in the fall. Mr. Neary stated that he filed a petition to remove the trustee last week, that Mr. Capko is a beneficiary of a sizeable bequest which they are refusing to pay, that they learned that some of Mr. Capko's specific assets have been dispensed with (given away/thrown away/sold), and that he provided courtesy copies to the other side. Ms. Calhoun stated that she addressed all of the issues that were raised in the probate examiner's notes with a supplement that was presented for filing yesterday. The Verified Probate 850 Petition, etc. is continued to September 13, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A brief status report is to be filed at least one week before the hearing.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Xiao. Reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR #13714, 415-601-7054, apourtabib@gmail.com
